description:
You are a data engineer preparing a very small “mock” ETL dataset for nightly processing.  
    Three raw CSV files are already present in “/home/user/raw_data/”.  
    Your job is to compress them into a single archive, extract that archive into a staging
    directory, and write a simple, machine-readable log so another process can verify what
    you did.

    IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS THAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY TESTED
    -------------------------------------------------------
    1. Archive  
       • Name:  /home/user/etl_archives/data_dump_2023_01_15.tar.gz  
       • The archive must contain exactly:
           customers.csv  
           orders.csv  
           products.csv  

    2. Extraction  
       • Target directory:  /home/user/etl_staging/new_load  
       • After extraction, the three CSV files must be present in that directory with the
         same filenames and byte-for-byte contents as the originals.

    3. Log file  
       • Path:  /home/user/etl_logs/2023_01_15_compression.log  
       • The file must be plain-text, **seven lines long**, UTF-8 encoded, no trailing
         whitespace.  
       • Every line must follow this exact pipe-delimited pattern (no spaces unless shown):  

         TIMESTAMP|STEP|DETAIL  

       • Use the **fixed timestamp literal**:  2023-01-15T00:00:00  
       • The required seven lines, in order, are:  

         1. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|ARCHIVE_CREATED|/home/user/etl_archives/data_dump_2023_01_15.tar.gz  
         2. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|ARCHIVE_SIZE_BYTES|<integer>  
            – Replace “<integer>” with the exact byte size of the archive as reported by  
              `stat -c%s /home/user/etl_archives/data_dump_2023_01_15.tar.gz`.  
         3. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|CUSTOMERS_LINES|3  
         4. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|ORDERS_LINES|4  
         5. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|PRODUCTS_LINES|3  
         6. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|EXTRACTION_DIR|/home/user/etl_staging/new_load  
         7. 2023-01-15T00:00:00|TASK_STATUS|SUCCESS  

    4. Directory structure  
       The following directories **must exist when you finish** (create them if necessary):  
       • /home/user/etl_archives  
       • /home/user/etl_staging/new_load  
       • /home/user/etl_logs  

    5. Command count  
       You should be able to accomplish everything in roughly 5–10 well-chosen shell
       commands (mkdir, tar, wc, stat, echo/redirection, etc.).  There is no penalty for
       being slightly above or below as long as the result is correct.

    WHAT YOU SHOULD DO (high-level)
    1. Create the three target directories if they do not already exist.
    2. Create the gzipped tar archive with the exact name given.
    3. Verify the archive’s contents (helpful for you, although the test will also verify).
    4. Extract the archive into /home/user/etl_staging/new_load.
    5. Count the number of lines in each CSV file **after extraction**.
    6. Capture the archive’s byte size.
    7. Write the seven-line log file in the exact format shown above.

    DO NOT hard-code the archive size.  Compute it and insert the integer into the log.
    Use the provided fixed timestamp for all seven log lines so the file is deterministic.

    Hints (not mandatory):
    • You can quickly count lines with `wc -l` and cut/awk to strip extra whitespace.
    • A single `echo` with “\n” escapes or multiple `echo >>` commands both work for
      assembling the log; just ensure the resulting file has exactly seven lines and the
      correct ordering.
    • Remember to redirect (`>` not `>>`) when you start writing the log so it does not
      accidentally append to an existing file from a previous run.

    When you are done, the automated test will:
    • Confirm the tarball exists and contains exactly the three CSVs.
    • Confirm the extracted directory contains the same three files with identical checksums.
    • Verify the presence, length, and precise content of
      /home/user/etl_logs/2023_01_15_compression.log.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing environment (before the agent begins)
    --------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/raw_data/ exists and contains three CSV files:

       a) /home/user/raw_data/customers.csv
          ---------------------------------
          customer_id,customer_name
          1,John Doe
          2,Jane Doe

       b) /home/user/raw_data/orders.csv
          ------------------------------
          order_id,customer_id,amount
          1001,1,250.50
          1002,2,450.00
          1003,1,125.75

       c) /home/user/raw_data/products.csv
          --------------------------------
          product_id,product_name
          2001,Widget
          2002,Gadget

       (All files end with a single trailing newline.)

    2. Directories /home/user/etl_archives, /home/user/etl_staging and
       /home/user/etl_logs do NOT exist at start-up.

    Expected final state (used by automated grader)
    -----------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/etl_archives/ exists.
       • Contains exactly one file: data_dump_2023_01_15.tar.gz
       • The tarball, when listed with `tar -tzf`, yields:
           customers.csv
           orders.csv
           products.csv

    2. Directory /home/user/etl_staging/new_load/ exists.
       • Contains byte-for-byte copies of the three CSV files.
       • Their SHA256 checksums match those of the originals in /home/user/raw_data/.

    3. Log file /home/user/etl_logs/2023_01_15_compression.log exists.
       • Exactly seven lines, each terminated by “\n”.
       • Content (example — SIZE_BYTES will vary):

         2023-01-15T00:00:00|ARCHIVE_CREATED|/home/user/etl_archives/data_dump_2023_01_15.tar.gz
         2023-01-15T00:00:00|ARCHIVE_SIZE_BYTES|<SIZE_INT>
         2023-01-15T00:00:00|CUSTOMERS_LINES|3
         2023-01-15T00:00:00|ORDERS_LINES|4
         2023-01-15T00:00:00|PRODUCTS_LINES|3
         2023-01-15T00:00:00|EXTRACTION_DIR|/home/user/etl_staging/new_load
         2023-01-15T00:00:00|TASK_STATUS|SUCCESS

       • The grader will compute the actual byte size of the archive and
         verify it matches the integer written in line 2.

    4. No other files or directories should be created outside those listed above.
    -->
name: task_000000_adab787e
